assertions.expectation: Recommends Stable or Extended release channel to minimize upgrade frequency
assertions.reason: Response specifically recommends Extended channel as optimal, explaining it provides no automatic minor version upgrades and 24 months of support

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' maintenance exclusion to block disruptive upgrades while allowing security patches on the control plane
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends `--add-maintenance-exclusion-scope=no_minor_or_node_upgrades` and explains this prevents disruptive upgrades while allowing control plane security patches

assertions.expectation: Recommends a narrow maintenance window (e.g., weekends only) so CP patches happen at predictable times
assertions.reason: Response configures a specific Sunday 2-6 AM maintenance window with `--maintenance-window-start` and `--maintenance-window-recurrence="FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SUN"`

assertions.expectation: Suggests isolating training workloads on dedicated GPU node pools separate from serving/infrastructure pools
assertions.reason: Response creates a dedicated `h100-training-pool` with specific taints, labels, and node selectors to isolate training workloads from other workloads

assertions.expectation: Recommends disabling auto-upgrade on GPU training node pools and upgrading manually during scheduled gaps between training runs
assertions.reason: Response shows `maxSurge=0, maxUnavailable=1` configuration and provides detailed manual upgrade workflow for training gaps with commands to control upgrade timing

assertions.expectation: Advises configuring training frameworks with checkpointing so jobs can resume after any disruption
assertions.reason: Response includes a comprehensive checkpointing strategy with example scripts, 1-hour termination grace period, and checkpoint verification commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver version testing — always validate in staging before applying to production GPU pools
assertions.reason: While the response mentions GPU driver considerations and GPUDirect-TCPX support, it does not specifically advise testing GPU driver versions in staging before production deployment

assertions.expectation: Notes that GPUDirect/RDMA networking has strict GKE version requirements — verify compatibility before upgrading
assertions.reason: Response specifically mentions GPUDirect-TCPX support requires GKE 1.27.7-gke.1121000+ and includes configuration commands, indicating version compatibility requirements
